authors: Yaoqi Guo, Ying Xiao, Jie M. Zhang, Mark Harman, Yiling Lou, Yang Liu, Zhenpeng Chen
title: EET: Experience-Driven Early Termination for Cost-Efficient Software Engineering Agents
storage_format: Structured experience objects (JSON: issue_id, task_description, execution_summary, evaluation_result, confidence, confidence_reason); abstractions of successful resolution trajectories stored in an experience base
agent_framework: custom (EET — agnostic plugin evaluated on top of Agentless, Mini-SWE-Agent, Trae Agent)
benchmarks_used: SWE-bench Verified (500 tasks; 207 from SWE-bench Lite for experience generation)
performance_metrics: % Resolved, # API Calls, # Input Tokens, # Output Tokens, Total Cost ($)
baseline_compared: Agents without EET (Agentless, Mini-SWE-Agent, Trae Agent), Turn-control (Gao and Peng 2026), Naive-EET (RAG with raw trajectories)
input_tokens_per_task: Varies by agent: Agentless ~72K/task, Mini-SWE ~176K/task, Trae ~3.6M/task (without EET); average reduction of 29.9% with EET
output_tokens_per_task: Varies by agent: Agentless ~3.7K/task, Mini-SWE ~7.6K/task, Trae ~46.4K/task (without EET); average reduction of 25.1% with EET
memory_size_over_time: Experiences generated from 207 SWE-bench Lite tasks; base fixed for evaluation (does not grow during testing)
cost_per_task: Agentless GPT-5-mini: $0.028→$0.012/task (-55.1%); Trae GPT-5-mini: $0.324→$0.232/task (-28.2%); average -31.8% across 6 configurations
token_reduction: -29.9% input tokens, -25.1% output tokens, -20.8% API calls on average
main_result: EET reduces total cost by 19.3-55.1% (average 31.8%) across 3 agents × 2 LLMs, with negligible loss in resolve rate (maximum 0.2%). Early termination identifies opportunities in 11.3% of issues on average.
performance_vs_baseline: +2.7% average resolve rate vs agents without EET; Agentless benefits most (+7.5% abs with GPT-5-mini)
cost_vs_baseline: -31.8% average total cost vs without EET; vs Turn-control: -31.8% vs -41.4% (Turn-control reduces cost more but degrades resolve rate by -10.7%)
limitations: Evaluation limited to SWE-bench Verified; reliance on historical data for cold start in new domains; cross-agent transferability untested; experiences are specific to each agent
validity_threats: SWE-bench Verified as the only benchmark; GPT-5-mini and DeepSeek-V3.2 as the only models; experiences generated from SWE-bench Lite may not represent real production issues; threshold tuning on the same dataset may overestimate gains
